questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed

Parking reserved for disabled permit holders

Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red

Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction

Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead

An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median

Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck

Two lanes are merging into one

choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall

Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down

Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

The road ahead is divided by a median

Never drive in this lane

Sharp right then left turns ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What should you do when you approach a steady red traffic light?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light means you must come to a full stop and wait until the signal turns green and the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal means the same as which sign?
choices: A yield sign
A stop sign
A speed limit sign
A no parking sign
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: stop completely, then proceed when it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light indicate?
choices: Speed up to beat the red light
The signal is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
The intersection is closed
Pedestrians have the right of way only
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop if you can do so safely rather than racing through.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
choices: Triangle
Octagon (eight sides)
Diamond
Round
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always an eight-sided octagon, so it can be recognized even when partly covered.

questions.prompt: A flashing yellow traffic light tells you to do what?
choices: Stop completely before proceeding
Slow down and proceed with caution
Yield only to pedestrians
Treat it as a green light with no caution
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means slow down, stay alert, and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of the road's center mean?
choices: You may pass when it is safe
Passing is not allowed on your side
The road ahead is closed
You must turn left
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means passing is prohibited there.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign is used for what purpose?
choices: Giving route directions
Warning of possible hazards ahead
Marking a stop
Showing a speed limit
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs are warning signs that alert you to hazards or changing conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangle sign mean?
choices: Stop
Yield
Do not enter
One way
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, meaning slow down and give the right-of-way to others.

questions.prompt: What do broken white lines on a roadway indicate?
choices: Traffic moving in opposite directions
Lane changes are permitted when safe
No passing at any time

questions.explanation: Broken white lines separate lanes going the same direction and mean you may change lanes when it is safe.

questions.prompt: A green arrow showing with a red light means what?
choices: You may not move at all
You may proceed in the direction of the arrow
You must yield to all turning traffic

questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected movement in that direction even while the main signal is red.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, two vehicles arrive at the same time. Who goes first?
choices: The vehicle on the left
The vehicle on the right
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When making a left turn, you must yield to whom?
choices: Only to vehicles behind you
Oncoming traffic and pedestrians crossing your path
No one, because you are turning
Only to large trucks
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, when may you turn right at a red light?
choices: Never
After a complete stop, if it is clear and no sign prohibits it
Without stopping if traffic is light
Only when a green arrow appears
questions.explanation: Right turn on red is allowed in Missouri after a full stop when the way is clear and no sign forbids it.

questions.prompt: What should you do when an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on?
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the right edge of the road and stop until it passes
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping until they have passed.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
choices: The vehicle, always
A pedestrian crossing in the crosswalk
Whoever moves first
The vehicle turning right
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians who are crossing within a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When entering a roadway from a private driveway or parking lot, you should do what?
choices: Proceed without stopping
Yield to vehicles already on the road
Expect other traffic to stop for you
Honk and pull out
questions.explanation: Vehicles already traveling on the through road have the right-of-way over those entering from driveways or lots.

questions.prompt: How should you travel through a roundabout?
choices: Clockwise, yielding to no one
Counterclockwise, yielding to traffic already in the circle
In either direction
Stopping in the middle
questions.explanation: Traffic in a roundabout flows counterclockwise, and entering drivers must yield to vehicles already circulating.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal?
choices: At the moment you turn
At least 100 feet before the turn
Only if other cars are near
After you begin turning
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers time to react to your intended turn.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, what should you do?
choices: Always go first
Yield to vehicles already in the intersection or close to it on your right
Honk and proceed
Stop and wait for a police officer
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, yield to traffic already there and to the vehicle on your right when arriving together.

questions.prompt: When you want to turn left at an intersection, which lane should you be in?
choices: The far right lane
The lane nearest the center line going your direction
Any lane
The shoulder
questions.explanation: Left turns begin from the lane closest to the center line for traffic going your direction.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, what is the typical maximum speed limit on a rural interstate highway?
choices: 55 mph
60 mph
70 mph
80 mph
questions.explanation: Rural interstate highways in Missouri are generally posted at a 70 mph maximum speed limit.

questions.prompt: What is the three-second rule used for?
choices: Timing a yellow light
Keeping a safe following distance
Measuring stopping distance on ice
Deciding when to pass
questions.explanation: The three-second rule helps you maintain a safe gap behind the vehicle ahead under normal conditions.

questions.prompt: When driving in rain, snow, or fog, you should adjust your following distance how?
choices: Decrease it
Keep it the same
Increase it
Eliminate it to keep up with traffic
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility require a larger following distance to stop safely.

questions.prompt: The posted speed limit represents what?
choices: The minimum speed you must drive
The maximum speed allowed under ideal conditions
The speed you must drive at all times
A suggestion only
questions.explanation: Posted limits are maximums for ideal conditions; you must slow down when conditions are poor.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, what is the usual speed limit on a state lettered (supplemental) route?
choices: 35 mph
45 mph
55 mph
70 mph
questions.explanation: Two-lane lettered routes in Missouri are typically posted at 55 mph.

questions.prompt: Why should you avoid driving in another vehicle's blind spot?
choices: It uses more fuel
The other driver may not see you and could change lanes into you
It is always illegal
It slows down traffic
questions.explanation: Staying out of blind spots prevents collisions when other drivers cannot see you to change lanes.

questions.prompt: If you are being tailgated, what is the safest response?
choices: Brake suddenly to warn them
Speed up well above the limit
Increase your following distance and let them pass
Ignore it and continue normally
questions.explanation: Leaving more room ahead and allowing the tailgater to pass reduces the chance of a chain collision.

questions.prompt: What is the typical maximum speed limit on an urban interstate in Missouri?
choices: 45 mph
55 mph
60 mph
70 mph
questions.explanation: Urban interstates and freeways in Missouri are generally posted at 60 mph.

questions.prompt: You should be able to stop your vehicle within what distance?
choices: The distance you can see ahead
Twice the legal limit
Any distance, since brakes are instant
The length of one car
questions.explanation: You should never drive so fast that you cannot stop within the distance you can clearly see ahead.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it does what?
choices: Saves no fuel
Disrupts traffic flow and can cause crashes
Is always legal and safe
Improves visibility for everyone
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly forces others to brake and pass unexpectedly, which can cause collisions.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, which lane is generally for passing or faster traffic?
choices: The right lane
The left lane
The shoulder
The center turn lane
questions.explanation: The keep-right rule reserves the left lane for passing and faster-moving traffic.

questions.prompt: When is it never legal to pass another vehicle?
choices: On a straight, flat road with a broken yellow line
On a hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
On a multilane highway
When the vehicle ahead is slow
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited where your view is blocked, such as hills and curves, because oncoming traffic may be hidden.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, what should you always do?
choices: Only check your rearview mirror
Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder for blind spots
Honk your horn
Close your eyes and merge quickly
questions.explanation: A safe lane change requires signaling, mirror checks, and a shoulder glance to clear your blind spot.

questions.prompt: What must you do when a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and stop arm out?
choices: Pass quickly on the left
Stop and wait until the lights stop flashing
Slow to 10 mph and continue
Honk and proceed
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus loading or unloading children and wait until the red lights stop flashing.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when should you return to your lane?
choices: Immediately, before fully clearing the vehicle
When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Only after the next intersection
When the other driver waves you over
questions.explanation: Returning only after you see the passed vehicle in your mirror ensures you have enough room ahead of it.

questions.prompt: When another driver is passing you, you should do what?
choices: Speed up to prevent the pass
Maintain or reduce your speed
Move into the left lane
Brake hard
questions.explanation: Keeping or slightly reducing your speed lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: What does the rule 'keep right except to pass' mean?
choices: Always drive on the shoulder
Travel in the right lane and use the left lane only to pass
Stay in the left lane at all times
Drive in the center of the road
questions.explanation: Slower and through traffic should stay right, leaving the left lane open for passing.

questions.prompt: You should not pass at which of the following locations?
choices: A long straight section of open highway
An intersection or railroad crossing
A clearly marked passing zone
A road with a broken centerline
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited at intersections and railroad crossings because of cross traffic and limited sight lines.

questions.prompt: A center two-way left-turn lane (marked with yellow lines) is used for what?
choices: Passing slow vehicles
Making left turns from either direction
Parking during rush hour
Driving straight through to avoid traffic
questions.explanation: The shared center lane is only for vehicles preparing to turn left, not for travel or passing.

questions.prompt: When merging onto a freeway, you should do what?
choices: Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
Match the speed of traffic and merge into a gap
Merge at any speed and force your way in
Drive on the shoulder until traffic clears
questions.explanation: Accelerating to traffic speed on the ramp lets you blend smoothly into a gap without disrupting the flow.

questions.prompt: Under Missouri's Move Over Law, what must you do when approaching a stopped emergency or roadside vehicle with flashing lights?
choices: Speed up to pass quickly
Slow down and, if possible, move over a lane away from it
Stop in your lane
Maintain your speed and lane
questions.explanation: The Move Over Law requires you to slow down and change lanes away from stopped emergency and roadside vehicles when safe.

questions.prompt: Why are large trucks especially dangerous to follow too closely?
choices: They never use signals
They have large blind spots and need more room to stop
They always drive too fast
They cannot turn
questions.explanation: Trucks have big blind spots and long stopping distances, so you must leave extra space around them.

questions.prompt: How much road space should you give a motorcyclist?
choices: Less than a car since they are small
A full lane width, just like another car
Only half a lane
None, they should yield to you
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are entitled to a full lane, and crowding them is dangerous because of their size and balance.

questions.prompt: When you cannot see a truck driver in the truck's side mirrors, what does it mean?
choices: The driver can still see you
The driver cannot see you
The mirrors are broken
You are far enough behind
questions.explanation: If you cannot see the driver's mirrors, you are in a blind spot and the driver cannot see your vehicle.

questions.prompt: When a large truck makes a right turn, it often does what?
choices: Swings wide to the left first
Turns sharply from the curb
Stops completely in the intersection
Backs up before turning
questions.explanation: Trucks often swing left before turning right, so never try to squeeze between the truck and the curb.

questions.prompt: Bicyclists on the road have which of the following?
choices: Fewer rights than drivers
The same rights and responsibilities as other drivers
No right to use the road
The right to ignore signals
questions.explanation: Bicyclists share the same rights and duties as motorists and must be given room on the road.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you should do what?
choices: Honk to get their attention
Yield and give them the right-of-way
Drive around quickly
Expect them to wait for you
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog signals a person who is blind, and drivers must always yield to them.

questions.prompt: Why should you be cautious around slow-moving farm equipment on rural roads?
choices: It is always speeding
It travels much slower and may make wide turns
It has the right to pass you
It cannot be on public roads
questions.explanation: Farm equipment moves slowly and may turn wide, so slow down and pass only when it is safe and legal.

questions.prompt: At night, when following another vehicle, you should use which headlights?
choices: High beams to see better
Low beams to avoid blinding the driver ahead
Parking lights only
No lights to save the battery
questions.explanation: Use low beams when following another vehicle so your high beams do not blind the driver ahead through their mirrors.

questions.prompt: What is the best way to spot motorcycles before changing lanes?
choices: Rely only on mirrors
Check mirrors and turn your head to look at blind spots
Assume there are none
Listen for engine noise only
questions.explanation: Because motorcycles are small and easy to miss, always check mirrors and blind spots before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, an adult driver age 21 or older is legally intoxicated at what blood alcohol concentration (BAC)?
choices: 0.02 percent
0.05 percent
0.08 percent
0.10 percent
questions.explanation: A driver 21 or older is legally intoxicated at a BAC of 0.08 percent in Missouri.

questions.prompt: Under Missouri's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 can be penalized at what BAC?
choices: 0.02 percent or higher
0.08 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
Only at 0.04 percent
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 face penalties under the zero-tolerance law at a BAC of just 0.02 percent.

questions.prompt: What is the only effective way to lower your blood alcohol level?
choices: Drinking coffee
Taking a cold shower
Allowing enough time to pass
Eating a large meal
questions.explanation: Only time allows your body to remove alcohol; coffee, showers, and food do not speed it up.

questions.prompt: Under Missouri's Siddens Bening Hands-Free Law, what is prohibited while driving?
choices: Using the radio
Holding or physically using a cell phone
Talking to passengers
Drinking water
questions.explanation: The hands-free law prohibits holding or physically using a cell phone or electronic device while driving.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect your driving ability?
choices: It improves reaction time
It slows reactions and impairs judgment and coordination
It has no effect in small amounts
It sharpens your vision
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reaction time and impairs the judgment and coordination needed to drive safely.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for a commercial driver in Missouri?
choices: 0.02 percent
0.04 percent
0.08 percent
0.10 percent
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers are held to a stricter limit of 0.04 percent BAC.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your driving in addition to alcohol?
choices: Only illegal drugs
Prescription and over-the-counter medications
Nothing but alcohol
Only caffeine
questions.explanation: Many legal medications can cause drowsiness or impairment that affects safe driving.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to avoid driving while impaired?
choices: Drive slowly after drinking
Plan ahead for a sober driver or another safe ride home
Drink only beer
Wait 10 minutes before driving
questions.explanation: Arranging a sober driver or alternate ride in advance prevents impaired driving entirely.

questions.prompt: Which behavior is an example of distracted driving?
choices: Keeping both hands on the wheel
Texting or reading a phone while driving
Scanning the road ahead
Checking mirrors regularly
questions.explanation: Texting takes your eyes, hands, and attention off the road and is a dangerous and illegal distraction.

questions.prompt: If you feel drowsy while driving, what is the best thing to do?
choices: Turn up the radio and keep going
Pull over to a safe place and rest
Drive faster to reach your destination sooner
Open the window and continue for hours
questions.explanation: Drowsy driving is as dangerous as impaired driving, so stop somewhere safe and rest before continuing.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, when must you turn on your headlights besides at night?
choices: Only on weekends
When weather conditions require the use of windshield wipers
Only in tunnels
Never during the day
questions.explanation: Missouri law requires headlights on whenever conditions are bad enough to need your wipers.

questions.prompt: In fog, which headlights should you use?
choices: High beams
Low beams
No headlights
Hazard lights only
questions.explanation: Low beams cut through fog better, while high beams reflect off the moisture and reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: When does a wet road tend to be most slippery?
choices: After it has rained for hours
Just after the rain begins
Only when it is freezing
Never, if it is daytime
questions.explanation: The first few minutes of rain mix water with oil on the road, making it especially slick.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane, what should you do?
choices: Brake hard immediately
Ease off the gas and steer straight until tires regain grip
Turn the wheel sharply
Accelerate to push through the water
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering straight lets the tires regain contact with the road.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road usually freezes first in cold weather?
choices: Straight, flat stretches
Bridges and overpasses
Uphill sections
Gravel shoulders
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses freeze before other surfaces because cold air circulates above and below them.

questions.prompt: If your car starts to skid, what should you do?
choices: Slam on the brakes
Steer in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Steer the opposite way
Press the accelerator harder
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas and steering where you want to go helps you regain control of a skid.

questions.prompt: Before driving after a snowfall, you should do what?
choices: Clear only the driver's side window
Clear snow and ice from all windows and lights
Drive immediately to warm the car
Use only the defroster while driving
questions.explanation: Clearing all windows and lights ensures you can see and be seen in winter conditions.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your speed in heavy rain or snow?
choices: Drive faster to get through it
Slow down below the posted limit
Keep driving at the speed limit
Stop in the travel lane
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility mean you must drive slower than the posted limit to stay safe.

questions.prompt: If you are caught in heavy fog and cannot see, what is the safest action?
choices: Stop in the middle of the lane
Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Speed up to get out of the fog
Turn off all lights
questions.explanation: When visibility is too poor to drive, pull well off the road and use hazard lights so others can see you.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, you may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
choices: 5 feet
10 feet
15 feet
30 feet
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so firefighters can reach it.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, which way should you turn your wheels?
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning your wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, which way should you turn your wheels?
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
Either direction
questions.explanation: Turning your wheels away from the curb when facing uphill lets the car roll back into the curb if it moves.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, who is required to wear a seat belt?
choices: Only the driver
The driver and front-seat passengers, and children under 16
Only children
No one is required to
questions.explanation: Missouri law requires the driver, front-seat passengers, and all children under 16 to be buckled.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, a child under age 4 or under 40 pounds must ride in what?
choices: A regular seat belt
An appropriate child safety seat
The front seat
A booster seat only
questions.explanation: Children under age 4 or under 40 pounds must be secured in an appropriate child safety seat.

questions.prompt: Where is parking generally prohibited?
choices: In a marked parking space
Within an intersection or crosswalk
In a driveway you own
In a parking garage
questions.explanation: Parking in intersections and crosswalks blocks traffic and pedestrians and is not allowed.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into the street, you should do what?
choices: Open it quickly
Check for approaching traffic and bicyclists
Assume the lane is clear
Honk first
questions.explanation: Checking for traffic and bicyclists before opening your door prevents collisions and injuries.

questions.prompt: When you park on any hill, what should you always do in addition to turning your wheels?
choices: Leave the car in neutral
Set the parking brake
Leave the engine running
Roll down the windows
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake (and shifting to park) keeps the vehicle from rolling on a hill.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, a child age 4 through 7 who weighs less than 80 pounds and is under 4 feet 9 inches must use what?
choices: A rear-facing infant seat
An appropriate booster seat
A standard seat belt only
No restraint
questions.explanation: Children 4 through 7 who are under 80 pounds and shorter than 4 feet 9 inches must ride in a booster seat.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for people with disabilities only if you do what?
choices: Park there for a short time
Display a valid disabled placard or license plate
Turn on your hazard lights
Leave a note on the dashboard
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces require a valid disability placard or plate; using one without it is illegal.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, at what age can you apply for an instruction permit?
questions.explanation: Missouri's Graduated Driver License program allows you to apply for an instruction permit at age 15.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Missouri written knowledge test, and what score is needed to pass?
choices: 20 questions, 70 percent
25 questions, 80 percent
30 questions, 75 percent
50 questions, 90 percent
questions.explanation: The written knowledge test has 25 questions, and you must answer at least 80 percent (20) correctly.

questions.prompt: While driving with a Missouri instruction permit, who must be with you?
choices: Any licensed friend
A qualified driver at least 25 years old, licensed 3 or more years, in the front seat
No one, if you stay near home
A passenger under 18
questions.explanation: An instruction permit requires a qualified front-seat driver who is at least 25 and has been licensed for three years or more.

questions.prompt: How many total hours of supervised driving practice does Missouri require during the instruction permit phase?
choices: 20 hours
30 hours
40 hours
60 hours
questions.explanation: Missouri requires at least 40 hours of supervised driving practice before advancing from the instruction permit.

questions.prompt: Of the required supervised practice hours, how many must be at night?
choices: 5 hours
10 hours
15 hours
20 hours
questions.explanation: At least 10 of the 40 required practice hours must be completed at night, between sunset and sunrise.

questions.prompt: How long must you hold a Missouri instruction permit before moving to the next step?
choices: 30 days
90 days
At least 182 days
One full year
questions.explanation: You must hold the instruction permit for a minimum of 182 days before getting an intermediate license.

questions.prompt: At what age may you obtain a Missouri intermediate license?
questions.explanation: After meeting permit requirements, you may obtain an intermediate license at age 16.

questions.prompt: During which hours may a Missouri intermediate license holder generally NOT drive alone?
choices: Between 10:00 p.m. and 4:00 a.m.
Between 1:00 a.m. and 5:00 a.m.
Between midnight and 6:00 a.m.
There is no curfew
questions.explanation: Intermediate drivers may not drive between 1:00 a.m. and 5:00 a.m. except for work, school, or emergencies.

questions.prompt: Where must you take the Missouri vision, road sign, and written knowledge tests?
choices: Online at home
At a Missouri State Highway Patrol examination station
At any gas station
By mail
questions.explanation: These tests are given in person at a Missouri State Highway Patrol driver examination station.

questions.prompt: At what age is a full Missouri driver's license generally available?
questions.explanation: A full, unrestricted driver's license is generally available at age 18 in Missouri.

questions.prompt: In Missouri, accumulating how many points in 18 months results in a license suspension?
choices: 4 points
6 points
8 points
12 points
questions.explanation: Accumulating 8 or more points within 18 months leads to a suspension of your driving privilege.

questions.prompt: Under Missouri's implied consent law, refusing a chemical test results in what?
choices: A small fine only
An automatic one-year license revocation
A warning
No consequence
questions.explanation: By driving in Missouri you consent to chemical testing, and refusing one means a one-year license revocation.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of Missouri's driver point system?
choices: To reward safe driving with points
To track traffic convictions and identify problem drivers
To set insurance rates only
To measure miles driven
questions.explanation: Points are added for convictions, and too many can lead to suspension or revocation of driving privileges.

questions.prompt: What can happen after a first DWI conviction in Missouri?
choices: Nothing serious
Fines, possible jail time, and license suspension
Only a verbal warning
An automatic license upgrade
questions.explanation: A first DWI conviction can bring fines, jail time, and a license suspension, with harsher penalties for repeats.

questions.prompt: Missouri law requires drivers to carry what at all times?
choices: Proof of vehicle insurance
A spare tire
A first-aid kit
A paper map
questions.explanation: Drivers must maintain and carry proof of insurance; driving uninsured brings fines and points.

questions.prompt: What should you do if you are involved in a crash?
choices: Leave the scene immediately
Stop, check for injuries, and exchange information
Drive away if no police are present
Move on if damage seems minor
questions.explanation: You must stop at a crash, help anyone injured, and exchange information; leaving the scene is a serious offense.

questions.prompt: Driving on a suspended or revoked license in Missouri can result in what?
choices: No penalty
Additional fines, points, and extended loss of driving privileges
An automatic reinstatement
A reduced suspension
questions.explanation: Driving while suspended or revoked adds fines, points, and a longer loss of your driving privilege.

questions.prompt: When a law enforcement officer signals you to pull over, you should do what?
choices: Speed up to find a police station
Pull over safely to the right and stop
Ignore the officer
Stop suddenly in the travel lane
questions.explanation: Signal, pull over to the right as soon as it is safe, and stop when an officer signals you.

questions.prompt: Why do traffic violation points matter to a driver?
choices: They have no real effect
Too many can lead to suspension or revocation of your license
They lower your insurance
They expire instantly
questions.explanation: Accumulating too many points can cost you your driving privilege through suspension or revocation.

questions.prompt: A driver under 21 convicted of an alcohol offense in Missouri can expect what?
choices: No consequences
License suspension or revocation under zero-tolerance rules
A warning only
An automatic license renewal
questions.explanation: Under zero tolerance, drivers under 21 face suspension or revocation for alcohol offenses even at low BAC levels.
